reports.incident_description: Spot audit in Paediatric Ward showed hand hygiene compliance at 42%; several sinks lacked paper towels and one ABHR dispenser empty. Request QA-led corrective action and re-education.
After brief morning rain, wet entrance mats led to a patient slip near OPD triage (no fracture). Signage inadequate and housekeeping response slow. Needs QA review of environmental safety controls.
Two FBC samples from OPD were received with the same CR number due to duplicate ticket issuance. Near-miss averted by technologist; requests QA RCA on patient identification and registration flow.
Perioperative antibiotic prophylaxis given after incision in 3/7 morning cases. Breach of surgical safety bundle; needs QA audit and corrective action with surgical teams.
Found 14 adrenaline vials close to expiry still on primary shelf; FEFO not followed. NMRA compliance risk; requesting QA-led stock rotation audit and staff refresher.

Short afternoon rain revealed minor ceiling seep near Radiology waiting area; droplets fell close to an electrical socket. Please initiate QA safety investigation and preventive maintenance follow-up.
Patient complaint: token calling skipped numbers and perceived favoritism; waiting time exceeded displayed target by ~90 minutes. Request QA review of queue management and service standards.
Internal check found several BHTs missing the second identifier label (NIC/hospital ID). Breach of MoH patient identification policy. Needs QA compliance action across wards.
CSSD steam sterilizer load: physical parameters met but 2 trays failed chemical integrator. Instruments quarantined. Request immediate QA-led investigation and load recall review.
Evening humidity after brief rain led to wet packs post-ETO cycle aeration; risk of compromised sterility. Need QA to assess environmental controls and revise SOP adherence.
Complaint: Discharge summary provided only in English; patient requested Sinhala/Tamil as per hospital policy and MoH guidance. QA to review documentation standards and availability of translations.
Sepsis pathway activation delayed by ~25 minutes in ETU due to confusion over lab order entry and IV access prioritization. Request QA review of code sepsis SOP and drills.
CT head TAT for suspected stroke was 55 minutes (KPI ≤30). Delay attributed to porter unavailability and form re-entry. Needs QA process mapping and corrective action.
Waste segregation non-compliance: infectious waste mixed in general bin; one uncovered sharps found. Recurrent issue despite training. Request QA enforcement and spot audits.
Morning light rain caused damp OPD triage corridor; multiple near-slips reported, signage late. Request QA-led environmental safety risk assessment and wet weather protocol.
Vaccine fridge excursion to 11.2°C for ~50 minutes during rush; door repeatedly opened. Cold chain breach suspected. QA required for excursion handling and potential wastage assessment.
Fire exit by ICU blocked with oxygen cylinder trolleys moved indoors due to rain. Immediate life-safety violation. Request QA escalation and disciplinary action per policy.
Heparin infusion documented on nursing chart without a signed MO order in BHT; verbal order confusion noted. Needs QA review of medication authorization and documentation standards.
Lunch HACCP log incomplete; probe thermometer overdue for calibration sticker. Food safety documentation gap—request QA compliance audit and corrective plan.
Outdated consent form version (pre-2024) found in Ward 5 drawer and used for one minor procedure. Requires QA action on document control and removal of obsolete forms.
Analyzer QC for K+ out-of-range; 12 patient results auto-released before hold applied. Potential clinical impact. Request QA-led incident investigation and clinician notification protocol.
Misty morning condensation noted on portable X-ray detector; wiped and delayed start by 10 minutes. Seeking QA guidance on pre-use checks and EHS for early shifts.
Patient fall outside clinic where floor was wet from umbrellas during afternoon rain; minor abrasion treated. QA to open adverse event and review mats/signage/attendant allocation.
Rain-induced crowding led to long queues; six Category 3 patients waited >2 hours, breaching triage flow targets. Request QA analysis and surge staffing protocol.
Internal audit found missing monthly KPI submissions from ICU (mortality, VAP), surgical readmissions, and OPD satisfaction. Monitoring gap affecting accreditation readiness; corrective plan needed.
Two medication charts show overwritten doses without countersign; parents raised concern after nurse queried. Request audit of medication documentation and retraining.
Ceiling condensation above OPD triage dripped onto floor near scale; no caution signage deployed. Recommend RCA and environment safety controls.
Brief rain caused voltage dip; CT phantom QA failed tolerance, log unsigned and deviation not reported.
Internal audit found expired nursing competency files in Female Surgical; accreditation clause on staff credentialing breached.
Emergency laparotomy proceeded without documented WHO time-out; entry added later on BHT.
Vaccine fridge probe recorded 9.5°C spike; no corrective action form completed; OPD immunisation cold chain at risk.
Surgical Theatre 2 cleaning audit <80% twice; ATP swab fails; CAPA not closed.
Evening rain; wet lobby led to two near-falls at card counter; entrance mats and cone protocol not followed.
Near-miss: two EDTA tubes with same surname; LIS auto-merge override used; barcode policy breached.
Complaint: chest-pain case triaged Green at ETU; KPI review for triage accuracy and retraining required.
Anaesthesia machine leak test failed in Theatre 3; prior weekly checklist missing signatures; unclear handoff between maintenance and users.
Hot afternoon service saw low soup temperatures; carts lack calibrated thermometers; food safety standard non-compliant.
Mismatch between eBHT and paper BHT discharges; 14 records lack clinician sign-off; audit trail incomplete.
Thundery rain leak near RO plant; TDS spiked transiently; incident not escalated per SOP.
During rain, generator fumes entered OPD corridor via propped door; no recorded environmental risk assessment.
Two PPH events not logged in monthly audit register; M&M minutes pending; data gap in obstetric indicators.
Double-sided consent forms led to missing signatures on page two in five BHTs; informed consent process deviation.
ICU hand hygiene compliance fell to 42% overnight; dispenser in Bay 3 empty; need rapid PDSA cycle.
Morning drizzle left ambulance bay slick; stretcher wheels skidded; anti-slip mats still not installed despite prior recommendation.
Stroke rehab outcomes incomplete; mRS at discharge not recorded for Jan–Feb cohort; KPI at risk.
Temperature mapping overdue; one crossmatch dispatch used cool box without data logger; deviation not reported.
Adverse drug event (rash) noted in progress notes but not in incident portal; pattern of under-reporting in unit.
Gusty rain blew dust into Ortho Theatre ante-room; positive pressure alarm low; door discipline failure.
Power flicker with evening rain; two bedside monitors rebooted; crash cart defib post-check undocumented; staff unclear on SOP.
Instrument count discrepancy resolved only after re-open; count sheet had pre-ticked items; WHO checklist integrity compromised.

CSSD delivered several instrument sets with moisture inside after autoclave; high morning humidity noted. Packs quarantined, but this is a sterility breach requiring QA review of cycle validation and release criteria.
During the thunderstorm, water leaked into the 5B corridor; a geriatric patient nearly slipped and no caution signage was placed for 20 minutes. Request QA-led safety audit and CAPA with Engineering.
Power dip in the storm interrupted morning IQC, yet 14 CBC reports were released without documented daily QC. QA intervention needed for nonconformance handling and clinician notification protocol.

Rain blew into OPD entrance and soaked the complaint box; several forms are unreadable and timelines for response were breached. Request QA oversight to restore the patient feedback process and prevent recurrence.
Paediatric Ward hand hygiene dashboard not updated for 10 days; spot checks showed multiple noncompliance events. QA support needed to enforce the audit-feedback loop per MoH IPC guideline.
Negative-pressure in TB room fluctuated between 0 and -2 Pa during the afternoon storm; monitoring logs have gaps. Request QA to coordinate RCA and risk mitigation before next admission.
Look-alike medication near-miss not filed in the e-incident portal within 24 hours due to confusion with the new system. QA to retrain staff and ensure near-miss capture for trend analysis.
Two theatre consent forms lacked a second patient identifier and a witness; cases were delayed. QA audit needed on consent completeness and reinforcement of WHO checklist practice.
Due to rain, the waste yard flooded and yellow bags were temporarily placed near OPD with general waste; segregation and signage were noncompliant. Request QA investigation with IPC and retraining.
Damp BHT pages caused an insulin order to be misread; dose was corrected after telephone verification. QA to review documentation legibility standards and protective sleeves for wet conditions.
Antimicrobial stewardship audit submission for March is pending after registrar rotation; DDD tracking has stalled. QA to enforce compliance with MoH AMS reporting and continuity planning.
OPD satisfaction kiosks have been offline since Friday and no paper fallback was arranged, so no feedback captured. QA support requested to restore KPI monitoring and implement downtime SOP.
Post-thunder surge, triage documentation was incomplete; three Priority 2 cases waited over 60 minutes without timestamps. QA to conduct process audit and refresher on local triage scale use.
Time-out was marked not applicable in an emergency appendectomy though the team was present; a side-marking near-miss was later caught. Request QA-led RCA and re-briefing of theatre teams.
Vaccine refrigerator spiked to 9.2 C for 35 minutes during peak heat; alarm escalation did not occur. QA to review cold-chain compliance and alarm response protocol.
After a brief power flicker, LIS duplicated a potassium result in the EHR; a medication adjustment was almost made on the wrong value. QA to oversee change control with IT and issue a safety alert.
Needle-stick injury PEP was not initiated within one hour and the incident was reported only the next day. QA review needed for NSI protocol adherence and staff counselling process.
Heavy rain made the waste yard unusable; yellow bags remained in the corridor for over 6 hours with odour complaints. QA to verify storage time limits and set a rainy-day contingency plan.
Multiple complaints about wet OPD benches and absent warning signs; two elderly patients slipped without injury. QA to assess response time KPIs and signage availability.
M&M committee minutes for Jan–Feb lack action owners and deadlines; closures are not tracked. QA to implement a tracking matrix and follow-up mechanism.
Records room is damp with a mold odour and visible spotting on BHT files after morning showers. QA to initiate risk assessment, consult IPC, and ensure records preservation standards.
Buffet holding temperature recorded at 52 C during lunch and HACCP logs are blank for 24–26 March. QA to open nonconformance and conduct food safety retraining.
Sterile store measured 30.8 C and 75% RH in the afternoon heat; several peel packs are curling. QA to decide on quarantine and update environmental monitoring actions.
ETU entrance mat was saturated after heavy rain; a visitor slipped and sustained a minor abrasion. QA to categorize as a patient safety incident and coordinate CAPA with Facilities and Housekeeping.
March KPI dashboard shows denominator errors for SSI rate; figures exceed surgical admissions. QA to validate the data pipeline before submission to RHHD.
Heavy evening rain leaked into the records store; 24 consent forms damp and box storage not per policy. Request QA guidance on salvage, digitisation, and storage compliance.
Lightning-induced power dip caused the analyzer to reboot; QC not re-run before two STAT samples were processed. Downtime SOP adherence needs review.
Corridor outside OPD triage became slippery after the storm; wet floor signs were delayed and a visitor skidded (no injury). Please audit response time and signage readiness.
Generator auto-start lagged ~70 seconds during brief outage; monitors rebooted mid-resus readiness. Request RCA with Engineering and drill compliance review.
Afternoon humidity spike; Bowie-Dick borderline failure in Autoclave 2 and load quarantined. Need steam quality check, PPM logs, and corrective action.
Clinical waste overflow and mixed segregation noted since last evening; smell prompted patient complaint. Verify pickup schedule and staff compliance.
Token system froze after a power fluctuation; queues unmanaged >30 minutes and multiple complaints. Manual fallback SOP was not activated.
Downpour left the old lab stairwell wet due to louvre windows; anti-slip tape is worn and overdue for replacement per last audit.
Instrument and swab count section left unsigned on first case; corrected immediately. Request targeted compliance audit of OT documentation.
Storm-time restocking left vaccine fridge door ajar; temp rose to 10°C for 25 minutes and alarm was acknowledged late. Affected batches quarantined; review cold-chain SOP.
Orthopaedic ward hand hygiene audit at 52% vs 80% target. Need immediate coaching plan and repeat audit in one week.
Ceiling tile loosened with moisture in NICU corridor post-storm; droplet contamination risk. Admissions diverted for 20 minutes; environmental safety action required.
WHO Surgical Safety Checklist ‘time-out’ not verbalised for Case 3 until prompted. Request refresher and real-time checklist audit.
RO water check showed free chlorine 0.1 ppm pre-filter; sessions delayed and line flushed. Need CAPA, logs review, and vendor notification per standard.
Look-alike/ sound-alike error intercepted: ceftriaxone prepared instead of cefotaxime; barcode scan prevented administration. Request LASA mitigation and storage re-layout.
Lab report filed into wrong patient folder; detected on consultant ward round. Breach of two-identifier policy; needs retraining and spot checks.
Quarterly fire drill documentation incomplete; night shift in Ward 7 not oriented and uncertain of evacuation route. Accreditation compliance risk.
Sterile store RH >75% for ~3 hours during morning rain; some packs damp and wrap integrity compromised. Quarantined stock; HVAC and monitoring review needed.
Mortuary temperature logger shows a 3-hour data gap; probe battery flat. Chain-of-custody and accreditation nonconformance noted.
External QA blood sample mislabeled before dispatch; scheme informed. Requires RCA on specimen identification and corrective training.
Negative-pressure isolation room at -1 Pa vs -5 Pa target; door was propped open due to heat with a TB-suspected patient inside. Immediate containment and process review needed.
Patient slip in bathroom with superficial bruise; early mopping done without barrier tape though falls-risk signage was present. Review cleaning schedule and falls protocol.
OPD token display down again; manual fallback not announced, causing crowding and 12 complaints. Trend requires process improvement and monitoring.
Oxygen cylinder exchanges (n=14) lack batch numbers in the log; traceability broken and vendor slips missing. Immediate corrective action required.
Ward 5 patient satisfaction forms show identical handwriting/patterns suggesting coached entries. Request data integrity audit and retraining.
Ceiling leak during the afternoon storm wet the ICU corridor near oxygen outlets; no hazard cones placed. Request QA safety review and immediate corrective action with Engineering.
Rain-driven crowding caused triage overflow; fast-track signage unclear and two patients bypassed tokens. Please audit OPD patient flow and signage compliance.
Water ingress soaked several BHTs; some entries illegible. Risk to record retention and medico-legal standards. Request QA investigation and SOP reinforcement for document protection.
During heavy rain, OR humidity spiked >75%; two tray indicators showed incomplete sterilization colour change. Cases delayed. Need QA validation of HVAC monitoring and stop-rule adherence.
WHO surgical checklist ‘time-out’ was rushed due to storm-related schedule shuffle; antibiotic prophylaxis confirmation missed but caught pre-incision. Request QA audit and refresher.
Generator weekly test entry missing in the compliance log despite multiple power dips today. Please initiate QA nonconformance for critical equipment readiness.
Flooded corridor delayed OPD-to-Lab transport; three INR samples exceeded 30-min window. Pre-analytical quality risk. Request QA review of specimen transport during monsoon.
Vaccine fridge temp rose to 10°C for ~15 min after thunderstorm; alarm battery failed. Need QA investigation of cold-chain deviation and corrective actions.
Humidity caused OPD-label ink smudging on BHT charts; near-miss wrong-patient dose averted. Recommend QA check on label materials and labeling SOP.
Internal round found expired sutures in Ward 3 emergency trolley. Items removed. Request QA nonconformance and unit-wide spot audit.
Patient slipped outside X-ray after downpour; floor wet, no caution cone. Minor abrasion treated. Please initiate QA fall incident investigation and signage audit.
Adverse drug reaction form incomplete—key chronology fields left blank. Request QA-led retraining on incident documentation standards.
Hand hygiene observation: two interns missed ‘5 moments’ during lunch relief. Recommend QA feedback session and repeat audit next week.
Monsoon winds displaced lids; red and yellow biomedical waste bins found open and commingled in the yard. Please raise QA noncompliance and reinforce BMWM training.
Token display failed during storms; OPD patients queued blindly and lodged complaints. Seek QA review of communication contingency and patient information standards.
Due to prolonged rain, 12 histopath jars left in theatre anteroom ~3 hours before dispatch. Chain-of-custody and fixation delays. Request QA investigation.
NBM patient had wrong-colour ID band; nearly received oral meds during round. Near-miss flagged. Please audit ID band policy adherence.
Storm-related power blip moved machines to UPS; alarm histories for two patients not saved. Documentation integrity issue—request QA review of business continuity.
OR temperature 28°C and RH 78% for ~40 min; list continued without formal deviation approval. Need QA investigation into environmental parameter breaches.
ICU-to-ward transfer without medication reconciliation; duplicate anticoagulant order caught on round. Request QA audit of handover checklist use.
Tracer review found missing Q2 fire drill records for OPD. Please open QA CAPA and schedule make-up drill.
Leak over neonatal warmer during widespread rain; baby moved safely. High-risk facility issue—request QA-led hazard assessment and PM follow-up.
Cold room temp 10–12°C for ~50 min post-thunderstorm; logging gaps noted. Request QA verification of monitoring and escalation pathway.
Sewage backflow near pantry after intense rain; meal service paused. Food safety risk—request QA environmental risk review and corrective actions.
Month-end KPI extract shows missing Ward 5 discharge summaries; outcome metrics incomplete. Need QA data validation and escalation to clinical documentation leads.
During the morning downpour, water tracked into OPD entrance; mats absent, two slips and one elderly fall (no fracture). Request QA review wet-floor SOP and entrance risk controls.
CSSD noted several autoclave indicator strips not fully changed in evening cycle; humidity very high and packs were still released to OT. Need QA-led quarantine and sterilization audit.
Brief power dip after heavy rain; insulin fridge temp rose to 12°C for ~20 mins, no excursion form filled. Seek QA guidance on product quarantine and cold-chain documentation compliance.
Thunderstorm caused leak near scrub sink in OT-2; splash risk to sterile area. Recommend QA stop-list protocol and RCA on building integrity.
Complaint: consent taken only in English for endoscopy; patient states no Sinhala/Tamil explanation documented. Please check policy adherence and staff briefing.
Wind blew open clinical waste room; yellow bags briefly outside and one torn by stray dog. Noncompliance with waste segregation/storage; request QA audit and corrective actions.
Lightning caused short outage; monitors rebooted, 8 minutes of vitals not charted. Need QA review of downtime SOP and generator transfer testing.
Internal audit found expired diluent used once on CBC analyzer; results already issued on six OPD BHTs. Require QA to initiate recall, clinician notification, and CAPA.
Antibiotic bulk store damp; humidity indicator pink, no log maintained. Request QA implement humidity monitoring and storage compliance check.
Falls-risk form incomplete in 4/6 new admissions; one bathroom slip (no injury). Need QA spot audit and immediate feedback.
Multiple BHT discharges lack consultant signature and ICD coding delays; patients complain of insurance claim holdup. Please verify documentation compliance.
Rear stairwell fire door wedged open with bin due to wind; egress compromised and noncompliant with fire code. Request QA enforcement and action tracking.
Hand hygiene audit in ETU at shift change showed 38% compliance, frequent glove-on-touch. Recommend QA-led corrective training and HICC escalation.
Near-miss: heparin dose calculated using outdated protocol from shared drive; intercepted before admin. Need QA document control and version management fix.
Rainwater flooded staff corridor; trolley with sterile sets splashed at base, not clearly quarantined. QA to trigger product recall and traceability check.
OT HVAC logged humidity >65% during first case; no follow-up action recorded. Potential SSI risk—request QA immediate environmental check and case review.
Mid-month survey shows median OPD waiting >3 hours; multiple complaints on token sequence errors. Propose QA-facilitated QI project.
Rainwater seeped into records room; several BHTs on floor damp, covers stained. Need QA to activate records salvage SOP and assess confidentiality risks.
Medical gas manifold area labels faded/peeled after rain; new staff hesitated during cylinder change. Noncompliance with safety labeling—request QA audit.
Two vesicant extravasations this week via peripheral lines; vein assessment checklist incompletely filled. Please initiate QA-led cluster review and protocol reinforcement.
Unlabelled sample medicines found in clinic cupboard; not in pharmacy ledger, NMRA compliance issue. Require QA investigation and corrective action.
Ceiling leak above radiology waiting; damp near exposed wiring and patient seating. Area closed temporarily—need QA to classify as sentinel hazard and track fixes.
Indicator tape not adhering on wrapped sets due to humidity; IDs handwritten on wrap, traceability compromised. Request QA nonconformance handling and retraining.
Heavy coastal downpour left pooling at ambulance bay; stretcher wheels slipped, near-fall. Need QA-led safety walkabout and anti-slip controls.
M&M minutes lack action tracking; several last-month items unresolved. Request QA standardize governance templates and follow-ups.
Multiple chemical indicators failed to change on wrapped sets this morning; ambient humidity was very high. Request QA review of environmental controls and hold-release process.
Rainwater tracked into OPD corridor created a slippery patch with no caution signage for ~20 minutes; one visitor slipped (no injury). Please audit safety rounds and signage stock during monsoon.
Brief thunderstorm-related power flicker caused LIS downtime; two ETU samples were processed without applying downtime labels—traceability gap noted. QA review and staff retraining needed.
OPD feedback forms available only in Sinhala today; Tamil/English not stocked. Noncompliance with patient communication standards and accreditation requirements.
Seven instrument sets found damp on opening post-sterilization; steady rain and high humidity reported. Sets quarantined—request QA audit of cycle parameters and storage conditions.
Main entrance coir mats saturated from continuous rain; no extra absorbent mats deployed. Two near-miss slips reported—please review monsoon entryway SOP compliance.
Ward 3B isolation room lost negative pressure for ~12 minutes after a power flicker; alarm acknowledged but escalation chain unclear. Request QA investigation and corrective action.
Near-miss: two BHT labels mixed at Pathology reception; discrepancy caught before analysis. Recommend QA-led audit on specimen identification practice.
Audit shows 6/20 cases with surgical antibiotic prophylaxis given >60 min before incision in OT. Need QA-facilitated QI plan with theatre team.
Yellow clinical waste mixed with general waste behind OPD during light rain; bin lids left open. Noncompliance with segregation and infection control policy.
Dampness in Medical Records store; several BHT bundles on lower rack wet after last night’s rain. Risk to record integrity—request QA guidance on salvage and storage controls.
During thunder, central monitor feed dropped; manual charting not commenced for 18 minutes. Downtime SOP not followed—please initiate incident review.
Nine of today’s fifteen new admissions lack initial fall-risk assessment on BHT. Request QA to support rapid audit and corrective action.
Reagent fridge alarmed overnight; manual temperature checks not recorded from 12–4 AM (stormy night). Verify cold chain integrity and reinforce SOP.
Post-outage review shows generator fuel log totals don’t match hour-meter. Documentation control issue—request QA audit of records and reconciliation process.
During steady rain, crowd sheltering led to triage congestion; fast-track SOP not activated. Two formal complaints about delays—process compliance review needed.
Ceiling leak near Dialysis RO plant; humidity alarm triggered. Buckets placed but no incident form until prompted—please address reporting culture and environmental risk.
Severe rash after ceftriaxone not entered in ADR register and not reported to NMRA within 7 days. Regulatory noncompliance—request QA follow-up.
Hand hygiene spot audit in Surgical Ward: 58% vs target 80%; July trend declining. Request QA-facilitated corrective action plan.
Gusty conditions caused Theatre 2 door to draft open; sterile field wrapper lifted. Case paused—request environmental controls and door-closer compliance audit.
Moisture ingress seen at OPD injection room outlet after rain; monthly electrical safety checklist for area is overdue. QA to verify compliance and follow-up.
Cataract camp consents issued only in English; Sinhala/Tamil versions not provided. Several patients signed without translation—urgent policy and process review needed.
Patient satisfaction kiosk offline since Friday; no paper fallback used. Weekly metric gap—request QA guidance on continuity of data capture.
Dawn thunderstorm led to vaccine fridge rising to 10°C for ~35 minutes; deviation form initiated late. Cold-chain risk—request QA oversight for excursion assessment.
During LIS outage, two units were issued after manual crossmatch without second-person verification per SOP. Major near-miss—RCA and corrective action required.
Two ortho sets came out damp despite full autoclave run; CSSD humidity at 75%. Cases delayed—request QA review of sterilization parameters and environment control.
Several OPD clients asked how to lodge complaints; forms in Sinhala/Tamil not displayed and noticeboard outdated. Please verify complaints handling compliance and refresh materials.
Pre-op consent packs in Ward 7 lack Tamil version; some interpreter sections blank. Possible MoH bilingual consent non-compliance—request QA audit.
Brief rain led to wet lobby tiles; no caution cones placed. Visitor slipped (no injury). Near-miss logged—QA to review housekeeping SOP and corrective action plan.
Two EDTA tubes from ETU had identical barcodes but different names handwritten; near-miss prevented. Request QA-led RCA and labeling audit at ETU.
During thundery rain, seepage noted above Bay B; two cots shifted. Need QA to assess facility risk and track CAPA for leak source.
Power dip; generator stabilized after ~20 seconds. Three HDU monitors rebooted; no harm. Event requires QA review of power contingency testing frequency.
Patient for minor suturing arrived without ID band; only clinic card checked. Breach of two-identifier policy—request QA corrective training.
On ETU-to-ward transfer, medication reconciliation not done; duplicate cefuroxime charted then stopped. No harm. QA to audit handover checklist compliance.
Autoclave printer paper got damp during rain; two cycle records are unreadable. Traceability risk—please have QA enforce documentation controls.
Vaccine fridge logged 9–10°C for ~45 minutes in afternoon heat; frequent door opening noted. Potential cold-chain breach—request QA guidance on quarantine and CAPA.
WHO checklist ‘sign-in’ incomplete in OT 2; allergy status not ticked before induction. No adverse event. QA reinforcement of checklist compliance needed.
Gusty rain caused power fluctuation; patient on trolley trapped in Lift 2 for 7 minutes en route to CT. QA to review emergency lift drill and response time.
Rainwater entered biomedical waste holding area; yellow bag labels smeared. Environmental control breach—QA to coordinate corrective actions and compliance check.
Patient satisfaction forms in Sinhala exhausted at OPD; only English available. KPI collection affected—request QA to ensure tri-lingual supply and continuity.
Manual ticket-time register not tallied yesterday; waiting-time dashboard shows zero. Need QA support to standardize data capture and backfill.
Leak during thundershower dripping near 3‑pin outlet in X-ray waiting area; area cordoned. Please have QA log safety hazard and verify controls.
RO water TDS log has no entries for last 24 hours though sessions ran. Compliance gap—request QA audit and re-training on documentation.
Morning rush: NEWS scores not calculated; ‘stable’ ticked for several patients. Risk of missed deterioration—QA refresher and monitoring requested.
Night-to-day handover sent as WhatsApp voice notes instead of SBAR sheet; key details missing. Please QA to enforce formal handover policy.
Ward 2 crash cart seal broken; two adrenaline amps expired last month. Non-conformance found on round—request QA to expand spot-checks.
Scope drying cabinet showed RH >60% for about 2 hours in afternoon heat; scopes stored meanwhile. Need QA guidance on quarantine and environment control.
Thunderstorm power fluctuations disrupted SMS alerts; critical K+ 6.9 not notified for 25 minutes. QA to verify critical result escalation pathway and fallback.
Heavy rain caused pooling at OPD entrance; elderly patient slipped, minor abrasion. Incident logged—request QA-led RCA and preventive measures.
Weekend shift: HCAs entered isolation room without face shields; donning poster present but ignored. Please QA to conduct spot audit and reinforce training.
Monthly BHT documentation spot-check: 7/25 files had undocumented allergies and unsigned nursing notes. Request QA audit and corrective training per hospital policy.
Vaccine fridge recorded 10°C for ~35 minutes during stock take; heat today likely worsened drift. Possible cold-chain breach—QA to lead RCA and patient safety notification protocol.
Near-miss: Gentamicin 80 mg ampoule stocked in 40 mg bin; intercepted before dose prep. LASA control not followed. Request QA review of storage SOP and label strategy.
After rain, OPD foyer floor got wet; no caution boards placed; one patient almost slipped. Please trigger QA safety audit and immediate CAPA.
Two ortho sets failed chemical indicators; printout shows brief temperature drop and visible condensation after morning drizzle. QA to quarantine loads and review autoclave validation.
OPD billing queue breached 45-min KPI; token sequence skipped during manual override. Need QA process mapping and staff retraining.
Ceiling leak in surgical corridor during rain; sterile trolley staged nearby. Request QA-led risk assessment and compliance check with infection control standards.
August mortality audits not closed within 30 days; M&M minutes pending sign-off. Accreditation compliance at risk—QA coordination needed.
Thunderstorm surge—triage times exceeded 20 min for Category 2; two chest pain cases waited. KPI breach; please initiate QA review and surge protocol drill.
Two power dips; chemistry analyzer QC failed, but no deviation form raised. Need QA investigation into documentation lapse and contingency SOP adherence.
Segregation breach near Ward 5 pantry—yellow bag placed in black bin; staff unsure of latest MoH circular. Request QA audit and refresher.
Three consent forms in Theatre 2 missing witness signatures; discovered pre-induction. QA to enforce checklist compliance and audit sample.
Pressure ulcer bundle slippage—3 new Stage 2 within 48 hrs; turning charts incomplete. Request QA-facilitated RCA and prevention drive.
Patient slipped on puddle near lift after heavy rain; minor elbow injury treated. Please open QA adverse event review and falls audit.
Fridge temperature logs for outage period appear backfilled; same ink and handwriting for multiple shifts. Data integrity concern—urgent QA investigation needed.
Two empty O2 cylinders found on ready trolley; changeover checklist not signed at shift handover. QA to audit traceability and escalate to MOIC.
Weekly RO water quality test missed; outsourced lab delayed, no documented risk assessment. Noncompliant with RDHS guidance—QA action requested.
Patient complaint about privacy—no curtains at two examination cubicles; conversations audible in queue. Please engage QA on patient rights standards.
Covert audit: hand hygiene compliance 62%; family visiting overcrowding noted. Request QA-led rapid PDSA with unit leadership.
Storm water seeped into ground-floor MRD; several BHTs damp. Need QA guidance on document salvage and disaster preparedness compliance.
Sepsis bundle not started within 1 hr for two arrivals; antibiotics delayed; lactate not drawn. Require QA case review and pathway fix.
Humidity spiked to 78% for ~3 hrs during rain; dehumidifier offline; sterility risk for packed consumables. QA to evaluate and document CAPA.
Outage during rain—generator changeover delayed 4 min; weekly test log missing. Please initiate QA investigation and compliance review with accreditation standards.
Wrong-side prep avoided at time-out; booking form and BHT laterality conflicted. Near-miss—QA to reinforce WHO checklist reliability.
Two infant warmers found with expired PPM stickers; condensation on canopy in today’s humidity. QA to verify asset register and enforce PPM compliance.
During the evening thunderstorm, rain leaked onto the OPD triage area; one visitor slipped and fell. Wet floor signage was not promptly placed; request QA review and corrective action.
Brief lightning-related voltage dip caused two infusion pumps in Medical Ward to reboot mid-infusion. Near-miss noted; require QA-led RCA and UPS coverage check.
Sudden humidity spike during the afternoon rain; two sterilizer loads failed chemical indicators. Loads quarantined; need QA oversight on release and environment controls.
Internal audit found three consent forms missing documented risk discussion in Sinhala. Non-compliance with informed consent policy; request QA standardization.
Severe storm coincided with LIS outage; two critical potassium results breached the 60-min TAT KPI. Need QA review of downtime protocol and escalation.
Ceiling seepage during the thunderstorm; buckets placed near beds. Infection control risk; request QA environmental safety assessment.
OPD token display froze during heavy rain, causing crowding and patient complaints. Need QA to evaluate patient flow and backup process.
Pharmacy vaccine fridge recorded 10°C for 25 minutes after a power blip in the storm. Deviation logged; QA disposition and CAPA required.
Spot audit: 6/25 ED triage forms missing vital signs. Non-adherence to triage SOP; request QA retraining plan and re-audit.
Persistent rain tracked mud into ICU entrance; hand hygiene compliance dropped to 58% this morning. Need QA coordination for immediate controls.
Crash trolley check found expired adrenaline vials and dead laryngoscope batteries. QA to review checklist compliance and frequency.
Lift stalled after storm-related surge; 8-min delay transferring ASA IV patient to OT. No harm, but high risk; QA RCA requested.
Rainwater ingress dampened several inpatient files in Records. Need QA action on document preservation and disaster recovery.
ADR in Ward 5 not entered into e-ADR within 24 hours. Recurrent delay; QA to enforce reporting timelines and accountability.
WHO time-out not documented for an elective case labeled as urgent by team. QA to investigate checklist adherence and triggers.
Negative pressure failed in Isolation Room 2 after evening thunderstorms; smoke test failed. TB patient housed; urgent QA-led containment needed.
Due to squalls, porter route changed; four biochem samples arrived unlogged and warm. Chain-of-custody breach; QA to assess impact and process gaps.
PACS down during lightning; manual reports issued without second-reader verification in two cases. QA to review downtime checklist.
OPD satisfaction cards show sharp drop in waiting-time scores today. Token-counter mismatch suspected; need QA analysis and improvement plan.
Squally rain pushed water under OT changing room door, wetting the approach to the sterile corridor. Lists paused; QA to verify sterility and restart criteria.
Ward 6 audit: infectious waste mixed with general in three bins despite prior training. Recurrent non-compliance; QA corrective plan needed.
Thunderstorm triggered UPS alarm; one incubator temp dropped to 34°C for 6 minutes. Infant stable; QA review of power contingency required.
Lightning during torrential rain caused brief HIS/LIS desync, creating duplicate MRNs for two ED patients. QA to manage data integrity incident.
Roof leak over Station 3 during storm; water near power strips, two sessions postponed. QA to clear safety and coordinate maintenance.
This month’s M&M minutes list four cases without assigned actions or timelines. Governance non-compliance; QA follow-up required.

During the afternoon downpour, rainwater leaked into the sterile store adjacent to Theatre 2; several wrapped instrument sets were damp. Request QA to quarantine affected packs and audit building integrity and CSSD humidity controls.
Internal crash cart check found three expired adrenaline ampoules and one missing seal on the defib pads pouch. Nonconformance to emergency trolley policy; please log for QA corrective action.

Steady rain caused crowding and overflow seating at OPD; 9 written complaints about 4+ hour waits and token display visibility. Request QA review of patient flow and signage per service quality standards.
Brief power dips during thunderstorm led to two bedside monitors rebooting; no harm but alarms were temporarily suppressed. Please log as a near-miss and initiate QA-facilitated RCA with Engineering.
Random pre-list audit found four consent forms lacking a witness and one not available in Sinhala/Tamil per policy. Requires QA compliance review and refresher for surgical teams.

Samples from peripheral clinic arrived with rain-soaked request forms; two labels were smudged beyond readability. Pre-analytical risk noted; request QA to enforce secondary containment SOP with transporters.
Due to hot, humid conditions, two autoclave loads had wet packs despite indicator change. Halted release; seek QA guidance on environmental controls and load-drying validation.
Squally rain led to surge; Manchester triage steps were abbreviated and two yellow-tag patients waited 70 minutes. Need QA review of surge protocol and staffing escalation.
Token display went offline after a power surge; staff called names verbally causing queue confusion and three formal complaints. Please route to QA for service recovery and system reliability review.

Cold-chain fridge for thermolabile meds recorded 10.5°C for ~2 hours; deviation form incomplete and no documented product quarantine. Request QA assessment and CAPA for temperature excursion handling.

Continuous rain caused water tracking into Medical Ward corridor; a visitor slipped (minor abrasion). Wet floor signage absent; QA to lead patient safety review and corrective actions with Maintenance.
SSI surveillance shows rising LSCS infection rate past hospital benchmark for the last 6 weeks. Request QA-triggered audit of perioperative bundles and data quality check.
Quarterly QC records for lead aprons and shielding are incomplete; last documented test in July. Noncompliance with accreditation standards; please schedule QA internal audit.
Admission medication reconciliation missed a patient’s ayurvedic preparation; pharmacy flagged a potential interaction later. Near-miss reported; QA support needed to strengthen admission checklist.
Clinic satisfaction survey ran out; photocopied forms lacked full scale labels, compromising KPI comparability. Request QA guidance on tool control and data integrity.

Critical K+ 7.2 mmol/L communicated via phone during storm; no read-back documented and later follow-up was through WhatsApp without acknowledgment. Requires QA review of critical result communication policy.
Heavy rain caused a ceiling leak over the medication prep counter; IV admixtures were made nearby before area was cordoned. Immediate QA-led deviation and contamination risk assessment needed.
Morning rain forced OPD queues onto the steps; token lanes were unclear and pushing occurred. Please involve QA to revise crowd control signage and wet-weather flow.
Backlog of 27 discharge summaries >48 hours pending in HIS, affecting continuity of care and data reporting. Request QA follow-up on TAT compliance with units.
Infusion pump stickers don’t match the asset register; two pumps in Ward 4 show overdue calibration dates. Nonconformance to equipment QA requirements; needs reconciliation and recall if needed.
During thunder and heavy rain, warmer temperature alarm was silenced without escalation while staff attended another baby. Training and human factors gap; request QA-led review and refresher.
High humidity with ongoing rain led to under-dried linen; ICU received damp sheets. Infection control risk noted; request QA to implement release criteria and re-drying checks.
Observed late-list time-outs done verbally only; two cases lacked explicit allergy confirmation. Nonadherence to WHO Surgical Safety Checklist—request QA enforcement and feedback loop.
Two crossmatch samples from Ward 5 arrived without signed request forms; one lacked patient NIC. Recurrent documentation lapse; needs QA intervention with ward training and compliance monitoring.
Incident portal shows zero safety reports from Paediatrics for November despite known falls/near-misses. Likely under-reporting; request QA culture-building sessions and reporting refresher.
Afternoon thunderstorm caused ceiling drip near Ward 5 nurses’ station; floor became slippery and no caution boards available. Request QA safety review and signage stock check.
Brief power dip during thunder led two NICU monitors to reboot and freeze alarms. Please initiate RCA and verify compliance with electrical safety and generator switchover SOP.
Two ceftriaxone batches delivered with NMRA sticker-lot mismatch versus invoice. Need QA-led lot traceability audit and hold release until cleared.
After morning drizzle, several sterile linen packs felt cool/damp on opening, though indicators turned. Please audit CSSD drying parameters and quarantine suspect loads.
OPD patient complained Sinhala consent unavailable for incision and drainage; English form used. QA to review patient-rights materials and MoH language compliance.
Rainwater tracked from loading bay into meal assembly; floor wet near veg prep. Request QA food-safety assessment and corrective actions on drainage and door seals.
Due to evening rain crowding, visitors used side stair; handrail loose and no warning. Please arrange QA safety audit of escape routes and contractor rectification.
Microbiology received sputum without BHT number and ward label mismatch; near-miss caught. Seek QA reinforcement of specimen ID policy and ward audit.
Three BHTs discharged from Ward 8A lacked MO signature on reconciliation page. Request QA documentation compliance check and quick refresher for MOs.
OPD hand rub dispensers empty in three bays during humid rush; observed compliance drop. Need QA to run rapid PDSA and restocking audit.
Triage timestamps missing in HIS for 25 minutes during thunder-related network outage. Please verify QA downtime SOP adherence and data back-entry.
Lift stopped unevenly at 2nd floor; patient’s walker caught momentarily, no injury. Request QA safety review and vendor compliance check.
Transfusion request received with allergy field blank; O-neg emergency issue halted at bench. Urgently require QA-led RCA and ward retraining.
Two dialyzers found past expiry on open shelf; quarantined before use. Please conduct QA/pharmacy stock-rotation audit and revise bin labelling.
Original OT consent missing; only faint scan in MRD file for today’s list. QA to assess accreditation risk and enforce pre-op document verification.
OPD ECG machine showed leakage-current alarm after humid morning; removed from service. Request QA to coordinate safety checks and preventive maintenance audit.
Lobby mats saturated post-rain; detergent residue left floor slick and signage inadequate. Please review wet-floor SOP with QA and monitor compliance.
This week’s neonatal hypoglycaemia rates higher than baseline on SCBU dashboard. Request QA to validate data and convene case review.
Artery forceps missing from set; count discrepancy noticed only on return to CSSD. QA to audit surgical count process and theatre adherence.
Light rain made OPD entrance tiles wet; cones only in English, not Sinhala/Tamil. Please standardize multilingual safety signage via QA.
Formula fridge in SCBU shows overnight temperature gaps; data logger off. Need QA cold-chain deviation review and corrective actions.
Two recalled antibiotic lots still in store pending quarantine labels; NMRA notice not logged in QA file. Request immediate QA-led recall compliance check.
Humidity made X-ray room floor tacky; antistatic mats worn, artefacts seen on plate. Please initiate QA environmental control and image-quality audit.
Near-miss: insulin pen prepared for wrong patient; second-check caught before dosing. Urgently need QA RCA and immediate re-training on ID protocol.
Patient satisfaction KPI board shows data last updated in September; December feedback pending validation. Request QA to close reporting gap and publish dashboard.
